title: thymus serpyllum var. glaber
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. glaber, commonly known as glabrous thyme, is a variety of the thyme plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of glabrous thyme include its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiseptic properties, making it effective against infections and wounds. The health benefits of glabrous thyme include its ability to aid digestion, relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, and act as a natural antihistamine to alleviate allergies. Glabrous thyme can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a topical treatment for skin conditions and wounds.
